behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural translation output quality (grammar + semantic preservation), which a stub cannot validate. Rejected Workflow because the behavior describes a single model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model/API on a fixed multilingual eval set; assert human- or rubric-based semantic adequacy/fluency thresholds and detect regressions across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (API client error -> fallback path -> response shaping). In-process wiring with a stubbed API client returning 503 catches the real logic bug. Rejected System because no real network/OS behavior is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client interface + SQLite-backed fallback translator; force API client to return 503; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: This validates repository/SQLite integration, not just pure lookup logic. A unit test of mapping logic would miss SQL schema/query/binding issues. Rejected System because local in-process SQLite integration is sufficient and no external OS/network primitive is essential.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a real local SQLite test DB with known phrase pairs; invoke fallback translator through its service/repo boundary; assert exact stored translation is returned for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans computation plus persistence handoff; cross-module failure (calculator -> metrics store) is the boundary. Rejected Unit because 'and stores the metric' requires wiring. Rejected Agentic because this checks deterministic distance math/storage, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute translation flow with deterministic primary/fallback strings, verify computed character-level edit distance matches expected value, and assert metric is persisted in the configured store.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/priority + async side-effect behavior across local modules. It can be caught with wired components and deterministic stubs. Rejected Agentic because behavior text does not require judging model output quality, only precedence and background logging mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with both translator adapters and metrics logger; return deterministic outputs from both; assert response uses primary output immediately and verify background task logs/stores comparison without blocking caller.
